subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Guide the Fraction Compass crew as they match equivalent paths and compare fractions on bars, circles, and number lines.
lesson.mini_lesson: Equivalent fractions name the same point or same amount. To compare fractions, use equal wholes and attend to same numerators or same denominators.
lesson.objective: Recognize equivalent fractions and compare fractions with the same numerator or denominator.
lesson.concept_explanation: Fractions such as 1/2 and 2/4 are equivalent because they cover the same amount. With the same denominator, the larger numerator is larger. With the same numerator, the smaller denominator makes larger parts.
lesson.common_misconception: Learners may think different numbers cannot be equal, treat larger denominators as larger fractions, focus only on numerators, or misplace fractions on a number line.
lesson.real_world_connection: Comparing fractions helps with recipes, distances, and sharing fairly.
lesson.lesson_summary: Use models to find equivalent fractions, then compare same denominators, same numerators, or positions on a number line.

hints: Use equal-sized parts or the number line.
Same denominator: compare numerators. Same numerator: the smaller denominator is larger.
Use <, >, or = to compare.

story.text: Listen to this page. This mission introduces fractions as equal parts and numbers. Look at the whole, the equal parts, and the numerator and denominator in the picture. Think about how the mission story connects to the math. Count equal parts carefully before comparing or naming the fraction as you get ready. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This mini lesson teaches fractions as equal parts and numbers. Look at the whole, the equal parts, and the numerator and denominator and read the vocabulary words. Count equal parts carefully before comparing or naming the fraction one step at a time. Watch out for counting unequal pieces as if they were equal. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: This Watch page shows a worked example for Equivalent Fractions and Comparing Fractions. Look at the model and listen for how each part matches the question. Step one, read what is being asked. Step two, use the visual model. Step three, check the final answer with the right units or math words. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This guided demo lets you practice fractions as equal parts and numbers with help. Look at the whole, the equal parts, and the numerator and denominator before you choose an answer. Count equal parts carefully before comparing or naming the fraction and explain your thinking in math words. Do not rush past counting unequal pieces as if they were equal. Press Next to continue.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: Practice time. Use the model to help you work on fractions as equal parts and numbers. Count equal parts carefully before comparing or naming the fraction and check your answer. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Choose your answer when you are ready.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: Challenge time. The problem may need careful thinking about fractions as equal parts and numbers. Look at the whole, the equal parts, and the numerator and denominator, then decide which strategy fits best. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Answer carefully, then continue.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: Checkpoint time. Show what you know about fractions as equal parts and numbers. Read the problem, use the visual, and check for counting unequal pieces as if they were equal. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Choose or write the answer when ready.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: This badge page celebrates your work on Equivalent Fractions and Comparing Fractions. Look at your stars and remember the strategy you used. Mistakes helped your brain grow when you checked them. Press Next to see your profile.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This profile page shows how your mission went. Look at your accuracy, hints, and growth notes. If a level needs more practice, go back to the hub or continue to Skill Practice. Use your results to choose what to practice next.
profile.label: Read This Page
